source_paper_title: Improved Approximation Guarantees for Joint Replenishment in Continuous Time
context:
Page 33, Section 7 (Concluding Remarks), paragraph titled “The fixed-base setting: Approximation scheme?”

Toward further progress, one fundamental question is whether the algorithmic ideas behind \(\Psi\)-pairwise alignment can be leveraged to deal with the fixed-based restriction. Among other technical issues, it is still unclear how one should go about efficiently defining the approximate representatives \(\widetilde R = \{\widetilde R_\ell\}_{\ell\in A^*}\), as in step 5 of Section 2.4, while limiting their values to integer multiples of the prespecified base \(\Delta\).
title: Approximation scheme for the fixed-base continuous-time joint replenishment problem via adapting \(\Psi\)-pairwise alignment
background_latex:
Consider the continuous-time joint replenishment problem with \(n\) commodities \(i\in[n]=\{1,\dots,n\}\). Commodity \(i\) has fixed ordering cost \(K_i>0\) and holding parameter \(H_i>0\), inducing the EOQ marginal long-run average cost
\[C_i(T_i)=\frac{K_i}{T_i}+H_iT_i\]
when it is ordered periodically every \(T_i>0\) time units (under zero-inventory ordering).

A replenishment policy is a vector \(T=(T_1,\dots,T_n)\). Joint orders occur at times belonging to \(\bigcup_{i\in[n]}\{kT_i: k\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}\}\). For \(\Delta>0\), let
\[N(T,\Delta)=\left|\bigcup_{i\in[n]} \{kT_i: k\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}\}\cap[0,\Delta]\right|\]
be the number of joint order times in \([0,\Delta]\). The joint ordering cost has fixed cost \(K_0>0\) per joint order and is
\[J(T)=K_0\cdot\lim_{\Delta\to\infty}\frac{N(T,\Delta)}{\Delta},\]
and the total long-run average cost is
\[F(T)=J(T)+\sum_{i\in[n]} C_i(T_i).\]

In the fixed-base setting, a base time unit \(\delta>0\) is prespecified and feasible policies must satisfy \(T_i\in \delta\,\mathbb{Z}_{>0}\) for all \(i\in[n]\).

The paper develops an approximation scheme for the variable-base model (where \(T_i\) are arbitrary positive reals) using a technique called \(\Psi\)-pairwise alignment, which constructs a set of representative time intervals and reasons about their least-common-multiple structure; the paper notes it is unclear how to define such representatives while enforcing \(\delta\)-multiplicity.
problem_definition_latex: Problem: Design a polynomial-time approximation scheme (PTAS/EPTAS), i.e., for every \(\varepsilon>0\) an algorithm that returns a fixed-base feasible policy \(T\in(\delta\mathbb{Z}_{>0})^n\) with \(F(T)\le (1+\varepsilon)\,\mathrm{OPT}_{\delta}\), where \(\mathrm{OPT}_{\delta}=\min\{F(T): T\in(\delta\mathbb{Z}_{>0})^n\}\). In particular, resolve whether the \(\Psi\)-pairwise alignment framework can be adapted to the fixed-base restriction by efficiently constructing representatives \(\{\widetilde R_\ell\}\subseteq \delta\mathbb{Z}_{>0}\) that play the role of the variable-base representatives while keeping the approximation ratio \(1+\varepsilon\).
latex:
Consider the continuous-time joint replenishment problem with \(n\) commodities \(i\in[n]=\{1,\dots,n\}\). Commodity \(i\) has fixed ordering cost \(K_i>0\) and holding parameter \(H_i>0\), inducing the EOQ marginal long-run average cost
\[C_i(T_i)=\frac{K_i}{T_i}+H_iT_i\]
when it is ordered periodically every \(T_i>0\) time units (under zero-inventory ordering).

A replenishment policy is a vector \(T=(T_1,\dots,T_n)\). Joint orders occur at times belonging to \(\bigcup_{i\in[n]}\{kT_i: k\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}\}\). For \(\Delta>0\), let
\[N(T,\Delta)=\left|\bigcup_{i\in[n]} \{kT_i: k\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}\}\cap[0,\Delta]\right|\]
be the number of joint order times in \([0,\Delta]\). The joint ordering cost has fixed cost \(K_0>0\) per joint order and is
\[J(T)=K_0\cdot\lim_{\Delta\to\infty}\frac{N(T,\Delta)}{\Delta},\]
and the total long-run average cost is
\[F(T)=J(T)+\sum_{i\in[n]} C_i(T_i).\]

In the fixed-base setting, a base time unit \(\delta>0\) is prespecified and feasible policies must satisfy \(T_i\in \delta\,\mathbb{Z}_{>0}\) for all \(i\in[n]\).

The paper develops an approximation scheme for the variable-base model (where \(T_i\) are arbitrary positive reals) using a technique called \(\Psi\)-pairwise alignment, which constructs a set of representative time intervals and reasons about their least-common-multiple structure; the paper notes it is unclear how to define such representatives while enforcing \(\delta\)-multiplicity.

Problem:
Problem: Design a polynomial-time approximation scheme (PTAS/EPTAS), i.e., for every \(\varepsilon>0\) an algorithm that returns a fixed-base feasible policy \(T\in(\delta\mathbb{Z}_{>0})^n\) with \(F(T)\le (1+\varepsilon)\,\mathrm{OPT}_{\delta}\), where \(\mathrm{OPT}_{\delta}=\min\{F(T): T\in(\delta\mathbb{Z}_{>0})^n\}\). In particular, resolve whether the \(\Psi\)-pairwise alignment framework can be adapted to the fixed-base restriction by efficiently constructing representatives \(\{\widetilde R_\ell\}\subseteq \delta\mathbb{Z}_{>0}\) that play the role of the variable-base representatives while keeping the approximation ratio \(1+\varepsilon\).
